title: neopilea anivoranensis
intro: Neopilea anivoranensis, also known as the Madagascar jasmine, is a flowering plant species native to the island of Madagascar. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. The leaves and flowers of Neopilea anivoranensis are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
